idea.hook: Mysterious radio signals are flashing across the universe!
facts: For milliseconds, these 'Fast Radio Bursts' (FRBs) unleash colossal energy.
A single FRB can emit more power than 500 million Suns in one millisecond.
Originating billions of light-years away, their source remains unknown to science.
idea.payoff: Are they exotic cosmic events, or something far stranger?
idea.title: Universe's MYSTERIOUS Radio Bursts! 📡🌌

scenes.text: Mysterious radio signals are flashing across the universe!
scenes.visual: A dark cosmic background with a bright, ephemeral radio wave pulse expanding from the center. Faint stars are visible.

scenes.text: For milliseconds, 'Fast Radio Bursts' (FRBs) unleash colossal energy.
scenes.visual: A close-up of an intense, short-duration burst of light and energy radiating outwards from a central point. Text 'FRBs' briefly highlights.

scenes.text: A single FRB can emit more power than 500 million Suns in 1 millisecond.
scenes.visual: A stylized graphic showing a tiny, intense burst on one side, opposing a vast cluster of 500 million small sun icons on the other, against a deep space background.

scenes.text: Originating billions of light-years away, their source remains unknown to science.
scenes.visual: A rapid journey through a vast, star-strewn galaxy, receding into the distance, emphasizing immense cosmic scale. A subtle, glowing question mark hovers in the deep space.

scenes.text: Are they exotic cosmic events, or something far stranger?
scenes.visual: A mesmerizing, swirling nebula or an abstract representation of unknown cosmic phenomena, with a hint of a grand, perhaps alien-looking, structure faintly visible in the far distance.
